assignedTo.description: Person-ID strings for assigned helpers, may be empty

peopleNeeded.description: Number of people needed for the task >=1 (required unless taskId is provided)

priority.description: One of: high, medium, low (required unless taskId is provided)

roomArea.description: Room or area name (required unless taskId is provided)

scheduledDate.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) the card is scheduled for

taskId.description: Referenced backlog task ID. When provided, title/priority/roomArea/peopleNeeded inherit from the referenced task unless explicitly supplied.

title.description: Task title (required unless taskId is provided)

location.description: Where the error occurred, e.g. 'body.items[3].tags' or 'path.thing-id'

value.description: The value at the given location

$schema.description: A URL to the JSON Schema for this object.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.
examples: Property foo is required but is missing.

errors.description: Optional list of individual error details

instance.description: A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type. This value should not change between occurrences of the error.

type.description: A URI reference to human-readable documentation for the error.

room.description: Room or area the task belongs to

totalTasks.description: Total number of tasks in the backlog

unassignedTasks.description: Count of tasks with empty assignedTo

understaffedTasks.description: Count of tasks with non-empty assignedTo where len(assignedTo) < peopleNeeded

get.description: Returns a dashboard-ready daily schedule read model with date columns, available-helper counts, and ordered schedule task cards. The start parameter defaults to the planning-window start date (2026-07-05); clients should pass start explicitly for a different window.

parameters.description: Start date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to the planning-window start date 2026-07-05.
parameters.name: start

schema.description: Start date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to the planning-window start date 2026-07-05.

parameters.description: Number of days inclusive of start date.
parameters.name: days

schema.description: Number of days inclusive of start date.

get.description: Returns a combined payload with date-range metadata, summary counts, per-person daily availability, and a status legend. The start parameter defaults to the planning-window start date; clients should pass start explicitly for timezone-correct results. availableToday counts only people whose status on selectedDate equals 'available'.

parameters.description: Start date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to the planning-window start date.
parameters.name: start

schema.description: Start date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to the planning-window start date.

parameters.description: Number of days inclusive of start date.
parameters.name: days

schema.description: Number of days inclusive of start date.

parameters.description: Number of people to skip. Default 0.
parameters.name: offset

schema.description: Number of people to skip. Default 0.

parameters.description: Maximum number of people to return. 0 means no limit (return all).
parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of people to return. 0 means no limit (return all).

get.description: Returns a hello-world message confirming the backend is reachable.

post.description: Creates a new person with a server-assigned sequential ID.

delete.description: Deletes a person. Returns 409 Conflict if the person is referenced by backlog or schedule assignments.

put.description: Updates the name and initials of an existing person.

delete.description: Deletes a single availability entry for a person on a specific date. Idempotent: deleting an already-absent entry succeeds without error. Subsequent dashboard reads will fall back to the default-missing-availability behavior.
delete.summary: Delete person availability for a date

put.description: Creates or updates a single availability entry for a person on a specific date. The status must be one of: available, busy, partial, off. The date must be within the planning window.
put.summary: Upsert person availability for a date

get.description: Returns the global move range with startDate, endDate, and inclusive day count. The planning window is the canonical source of truth for the move timeline. All date-dependent views derive their rendered content from this contract.

put.description: Updates the planning window start and end dates. Both dates are required and endDate must be >= startDate. Returns the updated planning window with recalculated inclusive day count.

get.description: Returns all room and area records ordered by name.

post.description: Creates a new room or area record and returns it.

delete.description: Deletes a room or area record by ID. Returns 404 if the ID is not found.

put.description: Updates an existing room or area record by ID. Returns 404 if the ID is not found.

post.description: Creates a new schedule card with server-assigned sched-* ID. Returns 400 for invalid input or out-of-window date.

delete.description: Deletes a schedule card and its assignments transactionally. Returns 404 if the card ID is unknown.

put.description: Updates a schedule card and replaces assignments transactionally. Returns 400 for validation errors, 404 if the card ID is unknown.

post.description: Creates a new backlog task with server-assigned task-* ID and append sort order. Returns 400 for invalid priority, status, empty title, peopleNeeded < 1, missing room, or unknown assigned person IDs.

get.description: Returns a combined payload with derived summary counts, task backlog rows, a canonical priority legend, and a canonical task-status legend. The endpoint serves as the read-only source of truth for backlog task data used by the dashboard and /tasks route.

delete.description: Deletes a backlog task and its assignments transactionally. Returns 404 if the task ID is unknown.

put.description: Updates a backlog task and replaces assignments transactionally. Returns 400 for validation errors, 404 if the task ID is unknown.
